title: Cigno Australia Pty Ltd — Consumer harm
investigation_objective: The Federal Court ordered combined penalties of $7 million against Cigno Australia Pty Ltd ($3 million), BSF Solutions Pty Ltd ($3 million) and their respective directors, for engaging in credit activity without an Australian Credit Licence and charging prohibited fees under a 'No Upfront Charge Loan Model' that charged consumers more than $90 million in unlawful fees between July 2022 and May 2024. ASIC has since appealed the $7 million penalty to the Full Federal Court, stating it is concerned the amount provides insufficient deterrence.
scope: Federal Court found the company engaged in credit activity without a required licence and charged prohibited fees; Penalty imposed as part of a combined $7 million judgment across two companies and two individual directors; Consumers alleged to have been charged more than $90 million in unlawful fees under the lending model; ASIC has appealed the penalty as insufficient, appeal hearing not yet set
